overview: A stateful orchestration layer for AI coding agents designed to overcome the statelessness of tools like Cursor Agent CLI. It implements long-term memory, module ownership, a write-back learning loop, and multi-agent coordination using local file-based primitives (.mdc, .yaml, .md).

requirements.title: Ownership and Identity Registry
requirements.description: Establish a centralized system to define agent personas and map specific code modules to dedicated agent teams to preserve architectural intent.
success_criteria: The '.ace/agents.yaml' file correctly tracks agent ID, name, role, and responsibilities.
The '.ace/ownership.yaml' file successfully maps directory paths to agent IDs using longest-prefix matching.
The 'ace who <path>' command returns the correct assigned agent in < 100ms.

sub_requirements.title: Agent Persona Management
sub_requirements.description: Ability to create and list agents manually or autonomously.
success_criteria: CLI command 'ace agent create' adds a valid entry to agents.yaml.
CLI command 'ace agent list' displays all active agents and their metadata.

requirements.title: Modular Context Builder
requirements.description: A dynamic engine that composes a context 'slice' for an agent call by combining global rules, agent-specific playbooks, and recent session history.
success_criteria: Context synthesis includes global rules, the matching .mdc playbook, and the last 3 relevant ADRs.
Task-specific framing (implement vs debug) is correctly prepended to the final prompt.
The 'ace context show' command outputs the exact string to be injected into the executor.

requirements.title: Execution and RALPH Loop Motor
requirements.description: An iterative execution engine (Reasoning, Action, Learning, Progress, Halt) that runs agents until tests pass or maximum iterations are reached.
success_criteria: The system executes the 'cursor-agent' headless CLI with the synthesized context.
The loop continues automatically if the specified test command fails.
The process halts and provides a summary when the 'max_iterations' limit is reached.

requirements.title: Write-back Learning Pipeline
requirements.description: A post-task reflection process that extracts lessons learned (strategies, pitfalls, decisions) and updates the agent's .mdc playbook.
success_criteria: A reflection prompt is triggered after every task completion.
New strategies [str-XXX] and pitfalls [mis-XXX] are appended to the .mdc file without overwriting existing content.
Helpful/Harmful counters are incremented based on the reflection output.

requirements.title: Multi-Agent Coordination & Agent Mail
requirements.description: A system for asynchronous communication and consensus between agents when tasks cross ownership boundaries.
success_criteria: Agents can send and receive messages stored as Markdown in '.ace/mail/'.
The consensus protocol triggers a 'Human Escalation' if agents fail to agree after 3 debate rounds.
The 'arch-agent' (referee) can successfully parse and summarize debate threads.

sub_requirements.title: Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs)
sub_requirements.description: Formalized workflows for onboarding, PR reviews, and audits.
success_criteria: Running 'ace agent onboard' generates a subsystem status report.
Agents successfully flag YAGNI/DRY violations during 'ace agent review' tasks.

requirements.title: Operational Controls & External Integration
requirements.description: Manage operational costs through token modes and integrate visual design tools.
success_criteria: Token Consumption Modes (Low/Medium/High) adjust the depth of context and debate as per config.
Google Stitch integration successfully generates 'Vibe Design' prompts and imports resulting code snippets.
The 'ace config tokens' command persists settings across sessions.

requirements.title: Long-term Project Memory (AGENTS.md)
requirements.description: Maintenance of a project-root AGENTS.md file that acts as a global, cross-tool memory for all LLM agents.
success_criteria: AGENTS.md is updated with the latest 5 architectural decisions (ADRs).
The file contains a valid table mapping all agents to their respective .mdc rule files.
The 'Write-back' protocol section is present and clear for any external agents to follow.

requirements.title: Quality Assurance & Test-Driven Development (TDD)
requirements.description: Enforce strict testing standards including unit, integration, and agentic experience (AX) tests.
success_criteria: The system maintains 100% test pass rate on critical modules (Registry, Context Builder).
AX tests measure 'Context Relevance Score' and 'Write-back Accuracy' through automated verification.
CI/CD pipeline blocks merges that introduce code duplication or fail cyklomatic complexity thresholds.
